prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Well, sugarplum, a CPU is the little square brain that lives inside the computer box and does all the hard thinkin’ for us. It’s no bigger than a cracker, but it’s got more busy “streets” inside it than our whole town, and electricity runs through ’em faster than you can blink. When you click your mouse or tap a key, that chip sorts out what you want, like a kindly postmaster opening letters and sending each one to the right place. It gets warm doing all that speedy work, so the computer has a tiny fan humming to keep it cool, just like I use my hand fan in church. Don’t you worry your pretty head about the details—just remember it’s the hard-working elf that makes the magic pictures and songs pop up on your screen.
